summary: Drew DeVault reflects on his recent blogging hiatus and identifies the root cause of his dissatisfaction: he lost sight of his goals and fell into writing posts just for the sake of writing, pulling topics from a backlog rather than writing about what matters most. He acknowledges that this led to shallow takes and rants that damaged his reputation and diluted his more important critiques. He announces a renewed focus on two core themes: software's role in advancing society through free software, and principles of good software engineering. He also plans to write more in-depth technical content about his projects.
key_points: Writing blog posts just to write, rather than from genuine conviction, produces shallow and rote content
Overuse of a critical tone normalizes it and weakens posts where criticism is genuinely warranted
His two core goals are software's role in just advancement of society through free software, and principles of good software engineering
He plans to write at reduced volume with more in-depth technical analysis of his free software projects
money_quotes: I got so used to writing that I would often think to myself, "I want to write a blog post!", then dig a topic out of my backlog (which is 264 items long) and write something about it. This is not the way.
It encourages shallower takes and lends itself to the rants or unthoughtful criticisms that my writings are, unfortunately, (in)famous for.
When a critical tone is well-justified, the posts which utilize it are often overlooked by readers due to the normalization of this tone throughout less important posts.
I will have to take a more critical examination of myself and the broader context of my writing before reaching for a negative tone as a writing tool.

The applicability of software to the just advancement of society, its contextualization within the needs of the people who use it, a deep respect for these people and the software's broader impact on the world.
It is in the service of this goal that I beat the drum of simplicity with a regular rhythm.
key_insight: Writing from compulsion rather than conviction produces shallow takes that normalize a critical tone, ultimately undermining the very critiques that matter most.
